artPrompt: An evocative scene of civic resentment made magical: a glowing leafy tithe of green light siphons upward from a too-prosperous front yard, fence posts and a swollen pile of belongings shedding luminous motes that drift toward a gathering of ordinary townsfolk whose upturned faces catch warm ivory glow. Long dramatic shadows stripe the lawn, foreshortened from a low angle, golden afternoon haze. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Frontrunner Tax
text: At the beginning of each opponent's upkeep, if that player controls the most permanents at the table (you decide ties, charitably, in your own favor), they sacrifice a permanent of their choice and you create a 1/1 green and white Voter creature token.
flavorText: "It's not that we're against success. It's that we're against YOUR success, specifically, right now, loudly."
